ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or country of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., urban, rural, mountainous) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "date" TEXT /* The date for which the weather data is recorded */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was recorded */,
  "excess_rainfall" REAL /* The excess amount of rainfall */,
  "rainfall_factor" REAL /* A factor representing the rainfall */,
  "low_temp" REAL /* The low temperature of the day */,
  "mid_temp" REAL /* The middle temperature of the day */,
  "high_temp" REAL /* The high temperature of the day */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* The weather condition (e.g., sunny, cloudy, rainy) */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* The wind speed */,
  "humidity" REAL /* The humidity level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_records_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_data" (
  "temp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature record */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record */,
  "low_temp" REAL /* The low temperature recorded */,
  "mid_temp" REAL /* The middle temperature recorded */,
  "high_temp" REAL /* The high temperature recorded */,
  "temperature_unit" TEXT /* The unit of measurement for temperature (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_data_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rainfall_data" (
  "rainfall_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rainfall record */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record */,
  "excess_rainfall" REAL /* The excess amount of rainfall */,
  "rainfall_factor" REAL /* A factor representing the rainfall */,
  "rainfall_unit" TEXT /* The unit of measurement for rainfall (e.g., mm, inches) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rainfall_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rainfall_data_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the researcher is affiliated with */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study of the researcher */,
  "research_interest" TEXT /* Area of interest of the researcher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_users" (
  "public_user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the public user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the public user */,
  "interest" TEXT /* Area of interest of the public user */,
  "subscription_status" TEXT /* Subscription status of the public user (e.g., free, paid) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("public_user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather record being accessed */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of access (e.g., research, education) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_research_access_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_research_access_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather record being accessed */,
  "public_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the public user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of access (e.g., education, personal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_access_public_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("public_user_id") REFERENCES public_users ("public_user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_access_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* The version number of the data */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* The date when the version was updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the updates in the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data provided by the source */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record */,
  "import_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was imported */,
  "import_status" TEXT /* Status of the import process */,
  "import_status_code" TEXT /* Status code of the import process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)
